task_completion.description: Create the Python script file on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'run_observations.py' located on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/), either visible as an icon or open in a text editor/IDE.

task_completion.description: Write logic to read CSV, sleep, and send commands
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the code inside 'run_observations.py'. It must contain logic to open/read a CSV file, a sleep function (e.g., time.sleep), and an API call (via REST or cosmos-api) sending the 'INST COLLECT' command with a 'DURATION' parameter.

task_completion.description: Execute the Python script to command the spacecraft
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window showing the execution of the script (e.g., 'python3 /home/ga/Desktop/run_observations.py'). There should be output indicating the script is running or has finished running.

task_completion.description: Create the summary report JSON file on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'pass_summary.json' located on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/).

task_completion.description: Include the required keys and values in the JSON report
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of 'pass_summary.json'. It must contain exactly these four keys: 'plan_file', 'total_observations_executed', 'total_exposure_time', and 'execution_duration_sec' with appropriate data types.

integrity.description: The script execution and JSON report reflect actual wait times
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the 'execution_duration_sec' in the JSON report is a realistic non-zero float value that accounts for the 'wait_sec' delays, proving the agent didn't just hardcode a zero or skip the sleep commands.

integrity.description: The script was genuinely executed in the terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the terminal history to ensure the script was actually run and did not immediately crash with a syntax or import error.

integrity.description: Files were saved to the correct directories
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that 'run_observations.py' and 'pass_summary.json' are actually saved as files in the Desktop directory, rather than just having their contents echoed to the terminal.
